name: x-article-publisher-skill
capabilities: Provides guidance for publishing articles to X/Twitter
Offers patterns for social media content distribution
Supports workflow automation for Twitter publishing
Integrates with Claude Code for content management
Helps structure articles for Twitter format
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 4 potential patterns: 2 high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags and 2 low-severity 'hardcoded URL' flags. Upon evaluation, the 'cryptographic' findings are false positives caused by GitHub HTTPS URLs in metadata, not actual encryption code. The 'hardcoded URLs' are legitimate source attribution links (lines 4, 22) with no security implications. This skill contains only documentation with no executable code, making it safe for publication.
valueStatement: This skill helps you publish articles directly to X/Twitter. It provides guidance and patterns for efficient social media content distribution.
limitations: Requires X/Twitter API credentials for actual publishing
Twitter character limits apply to published content
Rate limiting may affect publishing frequency
Network connectivity required for social media integration

useCases.targetUser: Content marketers and social media managers
useCases.title: Content Marketer Automation
useCases.description: Marketing professionals can automate article publication to Twitter from their content management workflow

useCases.title: Developer Blog Sharing
useCases.description: Developers can automatically share blog posts and technical articles to Twitter audience

useCases.title: News Distribution
useCases.description: Journalists and publishers can distribute articles across social media platforms efficiently

promptTemplates.title: Basic Article Publishing
promptTemplates.prompt: Publish my article to X/Twitter with a summary link
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick publishing of completed articles to social media

promptTemplates.title: Custom Tweet Formatting
promptTemplates.prompt: Format this article for Twitter with hashtags and mentions included
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating engaging tweets with proper social media formatting

promptTemplates.title: Batch Publishing Workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me publish these 3 articles to X/Twitter with optimal scheduling
promptTemplates.scenario: Managing multiple articles for social media distribution

promptTemplates.title: Article to Thread Conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this long article into a Twitter thread for better engagement
promptTemplates.scenario: Breaking down lengthy content into threaded tweets

bestPractices: Always preview generated tweets before publishing to ensure accuracy
Include relevant hashtags to increase discoverability of your articles
Keep tweet summaries under 280 characters for optimal engagement
antiPatterns: Do not publish entire articles as single tweets - use thread format or summary with link
Avoid publishing without reviewing generated content for accuracy and tone
Do not flood Twitter with rapid-fire publications - space out your posts for better engagement

faq.question: Does this skill require X/Twitter API credentials?
faq.answer: Yes, you need valid X/Twitter API credentials to actually publish content. This skill provides the workflow and patterns for integration.

faq.question: Can I publish articles automatically without review?
faq.answer: While automation is possible, it is recommended to preview tweets before publishing to ensure accuracy and appropriate tone.

faq.question: What is the character limit for published content?
faq.answer: X/Twitter has a 280 character limit per tweet. For longer articles, use the thread format to break content into multiple tweets.

faq.question: Can this skill schedule tweets for future publishing?
faq.answer: This skill provides publishing patterns. Scheduling functionality depends on your X/Twitter API integration and tools.

faq.question: Does this work with both X and Twitter?
faq.answer: Yes, X and Twitter refer to the same platform. This skill works with the current platform API.

faq.question: Can I add images or media to published articles?
faq.answer: Media support depends on your API implementation. This skill focuses on text-based article publishing workflows.
